title: The Clash of Civilizations and the Remaking of World Order
author: Samuel P. Huntington

education: Yale University (BA, graduated with distinction at age 18)
positions: Albert J. Weatherhead III University Professor at Harvard University
Director of Harvard's Weatherhead Center for International Affairs
White House coordinator of security planning for the National Security Council (1977-1978)
Co-founder and co-editor of Foreign Policy magazine
President of the American Political Science Association (1986-1987)
notable_works: The Soldier and the State: The Theory and Politics of Civil-Military Relations (1957)
The Third Wave: Democratization in the Late Twentieth Century (1991)
The Clash of Civilizations and the Remaking of World Order (1996)
Who Are We? The Challenges to America's National Identity (2004)

book_context.origin: Expanded from 1993 Foreign Affairs article 'The Clash of Civilizations?'
book_context.response_to: Francis Fukuyama's 'The End of History and the Last Man' (1992)
book_context.historical_context: Post-Cold War period, dissolution of Soviet Union, rise of globalization

key_criticisms: Contradicted by empirical evidence (e.g., Russia-Ukraine war)

influence.academic_impact: One of the most cited and debated works in political science
influence.policy_influence: Cited by policymakers in the George W. Bush administration, Benjamin Netanyahu, Viktor Orbán
influence.political_movements: Adopted by right-wing populist movements including Donald Trump 2016 campaign
counter_movements: Alliance of Civilizations (Spanish and Turkish prime ministers, 2005)

related_books.title: The End of History and the Last Man
related_books.author: Francis Fukuyama
related_books.relationship: Intellectual foil; argues liberal democracy has triumphed

related_books.title: The World Is Flat
related_books.author: Thomas Friedman
related_books.relationship: Counterpoint; argues globalization creates level playing field

related_books.title: On China
related_books.author: Henry Kissinger
related_books.relationship: Nuanced view of Chinese civilizational identity

related_books.title: Orientalism
related_books.author: Edward Said
related_books.relationship: Critique of Western construction of the 'East'

related_books.title: Identity and Violence: The Illusion of Destiny
related_books.author: Amartya Sen

content_files.index.mdx: Book overview, summary, key takeaways, and metadata
content_files.01-content.mdx: Key concepts with mermaid diagrams, tables, and callouts
content_files.02-analysis.mdx: Strengths, weaknesses, criticism, and final assessment
content_files.03-narration.mdx: Audio-friendly flowing narration without headings or diagrams
